rows.locks: The Studio wizard accepts only Godot/Unity source-project paths, rejects shipped-build/decompiled-style paths before CLI routing, and routes imports through existing Rust migration CLI families.

The fidelity report view preserves Green/Yellow/Red rows, requires rationale/evidence, rejects Red rows without Era R tasks, and prevents lossy or behavior-bearing gaps from being laundered into clean status.
The demo and report validators reject claimed_ported_units and portClaimAllowed rows unless later oracle evidence exists; M94 keeps all behavior rows oracle-missing and port-claim forbidden.
The Studio demo summary records deterministic sha256 state-hash evidence from Rust-owned import reports, and the report validator fails when no IR/mapping/verification hash is present.

Studio Migration UX modules are control/presentation only: no File.write or :file.write/open calls in the lib surface, no new store, no artifact semantics ownership, and every write-affecting action routes through existing CLI/gates.
Coverage v82 records the migration UX contract, scripted demo, Rust-owned evidence boundaries, open #1/#23 governance anchors, and the exact verification command shape from #2189.

boundary: Scenario Coverage v82 is a Rust + Studio control-plane regression suite for Era O M94 Migration UX. It locks source-project-only import wizard routing, honest fidelity report rendering, Yellow/Red gap preservation, no auto-port or ported claim without passing oracle evidence, deterministic state-hash evidence, no trusted Elixir writes or artifact semantics, no new data store, no live bridge, no embedded engine runtime, no decompiled source copying, and #1/#23 open governance anchors.
